lessons.title: Introduction to HTML
lessons.content:
What is HTML?

HTML (HyperText Markup Language) is the standard language used to create web pages. It provides the structure and content of a webpage using a system of tags and attributes.

How HTML Works

Every HTML document follows a basic structure. Tags are written in angle brackets like <tagname> and usually come in pairs: an opening tag and a closing tag.

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
  <head>
    <title>My Page</title>
  </head>
  <body>
    <h1>Hello, World!</h1>
    <p>This is my first page.</p>
  </body>
</html>

Key Concepts

  • DOCTYPE: Tells the browser this is an HTML5 document
  • html: Root element of the page
  • head: Contains metadata (title, links, meta tags)
  • body: Contains visible content

lessons.title: HTML Elements & Tags
lessons.content:
HTML Elements

An HTML element consists of a start tag, content, and an end tag. Some elements are self-closing (void elements).

Common Elements

<h1>Main Heading</h1>
<h2>Sub Heading</h2>
<p>A paragraph of text.</p>
<strong>Bold text</strong>
<em>Italic text</em>
<br>
<img src="photo.jpg" alt="Description">

Block vs Inline

Block elements (div, p, h1-h6) take full width. Inline elements (span, strong, em, a) only take as much width as needed.

resources.title: What is JavaScript?
resources.content:
JavaScript Overview

JavaScript is the programming language of the web. It adds interactivity, logic, and dynamic behavior to HTML and CSS.

Where JS Runs

  • In the browser (front-end)
  • On servers with Node.js (back-end)

First Script

<script>
  console.log('Hello JavaScript!');
</script>

External Script

<script src="app.js"></script>
resources.cheatSheet:
## JavaScript Intro Cheat Sheet

### Syntax
- Inline script: `<script> ... </script>`
- External script: `<script src="app.js"></script>`
- Logging: `console.log()`

### Examples
```html
<script>
  console.log('Hello JS');
</script>
```
```html
<script src="main.js"></script>
```
```js
// app.js
alert('Welcome!');
```

### Common Mistakes
- Forgetting to include the `<script>` tag
- Placing scripts in the `<head>` without `defer`
- Syntax errors (missing quotes or parentheses)

resources.title: Variables & Data Types
resources.content:
Variables

Use let for reassignable values and const for values that won't be reassigned.

let count = 1;
const siteName = 'ProCode';

Data Types

  • String
  • Number
  • Boolean
  • Undefined
  • Null
  • Object
  • Array

Type Checking

typeof 'hello' //
resources.cheatSheet:
## Variables & Types Cheat Sheet

### Syntax
- `let` â†’ reassignable
- `const` â†’ non-reassignable
- `typeof` â†’ type check

### Examples
```js
let score = 0;
score = score + 10;
```
```js
const appName = 'EduPulse';
```
```js
const isLoggedIn = true;
console.log(typeof isLoggedIn); // boolean
```

### Common Mistakes
- Reassigning a `const` variable
- Using `var` unintentionally
- Confusing `null` and `undefined`

resources.title: Functions
resources.content:
Defining Functions

function greet(name) {
  return `Hello, ${name}!`;
}

Arrow Functions

const add = (a, b) => a + b;

Calling Functions

greet('Sara');
add(2, 3);

resources.title: DOM Manipulation
resources.content:
Select Elements

const title = document.querySelector('h1');

Update Text

title.textContent = 'New Title';

Create Elements

const li = document.createElement('li');
li.textContent = 'Item';
document.querySelector('ul').appendChild(li);

resources.title: Event Handling
Arrays & Loops
resources.content:
Create Arrays

const skills = ['HTML', 'CSS', 'JS'];

Loops

for (let i = 0; i < skills.length; i++) {
  console.log(skills[i]);
}

Array Methods

skills.forEach(skill => console.log(skill));
resources.cheatSheet:
## Arrays & Loops Cheat Sheet

### Syntax
- Array literal: `[1, 2, 3]`
- For loop: `for (...) {}`
- forEach: `arr.forEach(...)`

### Examples
```js
const nums = [1, 2, 3];
for (const n of nums) {
  console.log(n);
}
```
```js
const total = nums.reduce((sum, n) => sum + n, 0);
```
```js
const upper = ['a', 'b'].map(ch => ch.toUpperCase());
```

### Common Mistakes
- Off-by-one errors in loops
- Mutating arrays unintentionally
- Forgetting `return` in `map` callbacks

resources.title: Objects
resources.content:
Object Literals

const user = {
  name: 'Alex',
  role: 'Student'
};

Access Properties

user.name;
user['role'];

Update Objects

user.isActive = true;
resources.cheatSheet:
## Objects Cheat Sheet

### Syntax
- Literal: `{ key: value }`
- Dot access: `obj.key`
- Bracket access: `obj['key']`

### Examples
```js
const course = {
  id: 'js-basics',
  title: 'JavaScript Basics'
};
```
```js
course.duration = 12;
```
```js
const { title } = course;
```

### Common Mistakes
- Using dot access for keys with spaces
- Forgetting commas between properties
- Mutating objects that should be treated as immutable

resources.title: Async/Await & Fetch
resources.content:
Async/Await

async function loadData() {
  const res = await fetch('https://api.example.com');
  const data = await res.json();
  return data;
}

Error Handling

try {
  const res = await fetch(url);
} catch (err) {
  console.error(err);
}

Promises

fetch(url).then(res => res.json());

resources.content:

Build a small app with buttons to increment, decrement, and reset a counter.

Requirements

  • Display the current count
  • Three buttons: Increase, Decrease, Reset
  • Use event listeners
  • Update the DOM when the count changes

lessons.title: Lists, Links & Images
lessons.content:
Lists

HTML supports unordered lists (<ul>) with bullet points and ordered lists (<ol>) with numbers.

<ul>
  <li>Item one</li>
  <li>Item two</li>
  <li>Item three</li>
</ul>

Links

<a href="https://example.com" target="_blank">Visit Example</a>

Images

<img src="photo.jpg" alt="A beautiful photo" width="300">
lessons.cheatSheet:

      ` with `
    1. ` items - Links: `Text` - Images: `` ### Examples ```html

      • CSS
      • JavaScript
      ``` ```html Visit Example ``` ```html ``` ### Common Mistakes - Placing text directly inside `
        ` without `
      • ` - Using `target="_blank"` without `rel="noopener"` - Missing `alt` text on images

lessons.title: HTML Forms & Inputs
lessons.content:
Forms

HTML forms collect user input. The <form> element wraps input controls and typically includes <input>, <textarea>, <select>, and <button> elements.

<form action="/submit" method="POST">
  <label for="name">Name:</label>
  <input type="text" id="name" name="name" placeholder="Your name">
  
  <label for="email">Email:</label>
  <input type="email" id="email" name="email">
  
  <button type="submit">Send</button>
</form>

Input Types

HTML5 provides many input types: text, email, password, number, checkbox, radio, date, file, and more.
lessons.cheatSheet:
## Forms & Inputs Cheat Sheet

### Syntax
- Form wrapper: `<form action="..." method="POST">`
- Labels: `<label for="id">` + matching `id` on input
- Input types: `text`, `email`, `password`, `checkbox`, `radio`, `date`, `file`

### Examples
```html
<form action="/submit" method="POST">
  <label for="name">Name</label>
  <input id="name" name="name" type="text">
  <button type="submit">Send</button>
</form>
```
```html
<label for="email">Email</label>
<input id="email" name="email" type="email" required>
```
```html
<label for="bio">Bio</label>
<textarea id="bio" name="bio" rows="3"></textarea>
```

### Common Mistakes
- Missing `name` attributes (data wonâ€™t submit)
- Labels not linked to inputs (`for`/`id` mismatch)
- Using `GET` for sensitive data

lessons.title: Semantic HTML
lessons.content:
What is Semantic HTML?

Semantic HTML uses meaningful tags that describe the purpose of content, not just its appearance. This improves accessibility, SEO, and code readability.

Semantic Elements

<header>Site header / navigation</header>
<nav>Navigation links</nav>
<main>Primary page content</main>
<article>Self-contained content</article>
<section>Thematic grouping</section>
<aside>Sidebar content</aside>
<footer>Site footer</footer>

lessons.title: Final Project: Build a Profile Page
lessons.content:
đźŽŻ Project: Personal Profile Page

Apply everything you've learned to build a complete HTML profile page!

Requirements

  • Use proper HTML5 document structure
  • Include a header with your name as an h1
  • Add a navigation with at least 3 links
  • Create an 'About Me' section with a paragraph
  • Include an unordered list of your skills (at least 4 items)
  • Add a contact form with name, email, and message fields
  • Use semantic HTML elements throughout
  • Include at least one image
